title: Weak Acid ICE Table Solver
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Interactive ICE table that computes weak-acid equilibria, pH, pOH, and percent ionization with approximation checks.

learningObjectives: Construct ICE tables for monoprotic weak acids and solve for [H+].
Evaluate when the small-x approximation is valid versus when the quadratic formula is required.

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Guided ICE table visualization with numeric outputs
keyQuestions: How does Ka and initial concentration affect pH?
When is the small-x approximation acceptable?
commonMisconceptions: Ka large relative to concentration still allows approximation.
Percent ionization decreases when the acid is diluted.
assessmentOpportunities: Students capture ICE table outputs and justify approximation validity.
Learners compare percent ionization at two concentrations.
